parentQuestion: What type of cells line the ventricles of the brain?

scaffolds.q: The brain contains fluid-filled cavities called ventricles.
scaffolds.explain: The brain has four interconnected ventricles (two lateral, one third, one fourth) filled with cerebrospinal fluid. These aren't empty spaces — Continue the sequence: while I show you: they're functional chambers where CSF is produced, circulated, and eventually reabsorbed.

scaffolds.q: Which type of brain cell provides support rather than transmitting signals?
scaffolds.explain: Glial cells (neuroglia) are the support cells of the nervous system — astrocytes, oligodendrocytes, microglia, and ependymal cells. 'Support' undersells it, though — watch what they really do: they regulate the chemical environment, myelinate axons, defend against pathogens, and produce cerebrospinal fluid. Neurons get the glory, but glia do the engineering.
